[BODY]
Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch.

By the third day I realized it was scoring my own positioning, line by line. Not in a "look at this dashboard" way. The audit was telling me my About page describes the company in three substantively different ways across three pages. A reader hitting the homepage would not match what they read on the About page.

The audit was running on the same lens catalog we ship to clients. 149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames. The same scoring that would tell a fintech founder where their conversion funnel leaks was telling me my own brand narrative was incoherent.

Lesson: when your audit is good enough to fairly score your own marketing, it is good enough to ship.
[/BODY]
